title: Traffic and Free-Air Regime Indicators
summary: F1technical notes tyre degradation and overcut potential are strongly affected by traffic versus free air.
rationale: F1technical notes tyre degradation and overcut potential are strongly affected by traffic versus free air. Current models use Position and Position_Change, but not whether a car is likely stuck in traffic, in clean air, or able to extend a stint.
implementation_hint: For each race-lap, compute rank-normalized LapTime, Position density around current Position, number of cars within nearby positions, whether current lap time is faster/slower than adjacent position groups, and free_air_proxy for front runners or isolated position bands. Interact these with Compound, TyreLife, and RaceProgress.
expected_effect: May separate high-degradation traffic-induced early stops from low-traffic cars that can overcut or go long on older tyres.
risk: Without true time gaps, position-neighborhood proxies are imperfect; benefits may depend on whether Race/Position distributions match public test.
